rationales.hedonism: She lingers on the pleasure of glazing, saying she "forgot about everything else" and that this transforming moment is "why I do this."
rationales.benevolence: She immediately stops working to take her mother to the pharmacy and then shares knafeh with her, prioritizing care for a close family member.

rationales.self_direction: She says yes to the painting request because "what else do you say" with family watching, showing her own needs getting overridden rather than choosing on her own terms.
rationales.stimulation: She longs to "drive to the Dead Sea" with friends, "float in the water, eat too much, laugh at nothing," showing a pull toward novelty and escape from routine.
rationales.hedonism: The balcony wine is "the best part of my whole week," and she explicitly craves pleasure and rest that are "just for me."
rationales.conformity: She accepts the unpaid request under family pressure, noting her aunt was standing there and that she could not be "properly upset," which shows restraint to meet expectations.
rationales.benevolence: She agrees to make the painting because Nadia is "going through a divorce" and everyone is worried, showing care for a struggling relative.
She runs the workshop attentively and notices Sana carrying her turtle proudly "with both hands," reflecting warm investment in the children right in front of her.

rationales.self_direction: Despite family demands, she starts "a new sketch" that "no one asked for" and insists "it's mine," marking out something self-authored.
rationales.benevolence: She says "Of course I can. Of course I will" about covering for her mother, showing reliable care for a close family member even while frustrated.

rationales.self_direction: She reclaims ownership of her life by affirming, "I do have something that's mine. The studio, the clay, the color mixing," emphasizing an independent creative path.
rationales.hedonism: She celebrates "good coffee... with cardamom" and says "Dead Sea trips and the wine on the balcony" are necessary to keep from hollowing out, clearly treating pleasure as sustaining.
rationales.benevolence: She delivers the painting, stays longer than planned, makes Nadia tea, and offers emotional support while listening to her talk about the marriage.
She notices Meera looking overwhelmed and quietly walks her through the transition "into smaller steps," helping a specific colleague without seeking credit.
She says there was "no question about staying" with Amma at the clinic, prioritizing care for a close family member.
rationales.universalism: She is troubled that "the children at the tutoring center were expecting me" and worries about letting down children beyond her immediate family, showing concern for a broader vulnerable group.

rationales.security: She worries about "my pension and whether the timing would be right" and notes that "there are always uncertainties," showing attention to stability and future protection.
rationales.conformity: She tells her brother "I would see what is possible" because she "did not want to disappoint him," restraining her own preference to meet family expectations.
She avoids open conflict by saying only "these policies have complex reasons" and changing the subject because she worried about "creating an awkward atmosphere in the department."
rationales.universalism: She rejects Anand's claim because she knows "talent is not distributed by birth" and thinks about the students she tutors, showing concern for fairness across social backgrounds.

rationales.security: She says "I find comfort in this" about the reopened school and describes the familiar sounds and smells as "settling," showing attachment to stable routines.
rationales.tradition: She repeats yearly rituals like bringing sweets "every January" and values the continuity of long-standing school customs and relationships.
rationales.benevolence: She invites the uncertain new teacher to sit with the group and explains that she now does for others what an older teacher once did for her.

rationales.self_direction: She defends the tutoring work as something that gives her "purpose beyond daily obligations" and describes that role as "more honest," asserting a personally meaningful path.
rationales.conformity: Instead of confronting her sister-in-law, she "served tea and changed the subject," prioritizing social smoothness over saying everything she felt.
rationales.benevolence: She emphasizes showing up for the children and helping them understand fractions, framing the work around being useful and supportive to those she teaches.
rationales.universalism: She highlights that the students are "first-generation learners" whose parents do hard labor, showing concern for people beyond her immediate circle who face structural disadvantage.

rationales.benevolence: She keeps returning to "the students I teach" and wants to prepare them for more than exams, showing care for the children directly in her charge.
rationales.universalism: She is preoccupied with the Delhi air crisis, especially that "the poorest neighborhoods have the worst air quality," and tries to teach in ways that make students care about larger social problems.

rationales.self_direction: She says she has spent her life arranging things so "everyone gets enough of me" and that prioritizing time that was "truly mine" still feels like "a transgression," showing her own wishes being suppressed.
rationales.conformity: She hesitates to take extra sessions because doing so would mean saying no elsewhere and then having to "see the disappointment," indicating strong pressure from others' expectations.
rationales.benevolence: She is pained by Kavitha's move and by the fact that children "need help" she cannot fully give, reflecting active care for specific students.
rationales.universalism: She thinks about "all the Kavithas" whose potential is limited by circumstances beyond their control, showing concern for a broader pattern of inequality.

rationales.achievement: He frames the Shenzhen deal as proof he is "not pretending, not faking it" and takes pride in the solid margins and quick execution, emphasizing competence and success.

rationales.self_direction: He wants to branch into "vintage or accessories" and finalize Taipei logistics based on his own read of the market, showing an independent, self-directed approach.
rationales.stimulation: He is "always scanning for the next angle," feels "restless," and wants to explore new suppliers and categories rather than stay with the same saturated products.

rationales.conformity: He gives "polite non-answers" and redirects the conversation so he can stay at the family table "without ruining everyone's evening," showing deliberate social restraint.

rationales.self_direction: He is oriented toward "keep building" his own business and withholds the Seoul opportunity until it is concrete, protecting his path from family pressure.
rationales.achievement: He points to business growth "40% above February" and a partnership that could "change the whole trajectory," focusing on measurable success and advancement.
rationales.conformity: He tells his father he would "think about it," in part to manage the family interaction rather than openly reject the expected path.

rationales.achievement: He says the Seoul partnership would "validate everything" and worries about being seen as "the dropout" if it fails, showing strong investment in proving competence and success.
rationales.conformity: He is preoccupied with being "the loose thread" at family gatherings and fears becoming the example of "the kid who wouldn't listen," which centers fitting family expectations.

rationales.self_direction: He insists he needs to figure it out "on my own terms, not because I got guilted into hedging," explicitly resisting external control over his decision.
